name: The Mecca Center
mission: The Fund is formed as an endowment in support of the Muslim Educational Cultural Center of America DBA The Mecca Center
theoryOfChange: By providing comprehensive Islamic education (Quran Institute/Sunday School) and social support (Converts/Special Needs), the center aims to build a model Muslim character and strengthen civic responsibility.

keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 23/50, the organization shows high donor fit for the Muslim community through its zakat-eligible status and focus on religious education [2][4]. The score is balanced by the specific, localized nature of the cause and the absence of broader multi-year growth data [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The Fund demonstrates high financial health with a program expense ratio of 91.1% and 124.0 months of working capital [1][3]. However, the impact score of 31/50 reflects its role in capacity building rather than direct service delivery, with limited external evidence of specific educational outcomes [1].

strengths_deep_dive: The Mecca Center Endowment Fund excels in financial stewardship and institutional support. By maintaining <cite id="10">100% independent board members</cite>, the organization ensures objective oversight of its <cite id="11">$4,134,653 in total assets</cite>. Its primary strength lies in its 'Reserve Model' of operation, which provides a massive safety net of <cite id="8">over 124.0 months of working capital</cite> in reserve. This financial depth allows the supported programs, such as the <cite id="12">Sunday School and Quran Blossoms</cite>, to focus on educational excellence rather than immediate fundraising pressures.
rich_narrative.summary: The Mecca Center Endowment Fund serves as a critical financial pillar for the Muslim Educational Cultural Center of America (MECCA), focusing on the long-term sustainability of Islamic education and community services in the Chicago area. The organization manages a significant asset base to support diverse initiatives, including the <cite id="1">Mecca Quran Institute (MQI)</cite> and the <cite id="2">Mecca MVMT youth programs</cite>. In 2023, the fund demonstrated explosive growth, reporting <cite id="3">total revenue of $1,523,429</cite>, which represents a <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 179.4%</cite>. This rapid expansion allows the fund to provide consistent support for religious and cultural programming. The charity operates with high internal efficiency, maintaining a <cite id="5">program expense ratio of 91.1%</cite>. This ensures that nearly all funds are directed toward its core mission of capacity building for the community. Furthermore, the organization is highly accessible to the Muslim community, offering <cite id="6">verified zakat-eligible giving options</cite> that support specific categories such as the poor and needy.
